exemplar.title: Making a dress out of upcycled plastic to raise awareness about fast fashion

detailedExemplarData.title: Making a dress out of upcycled plastic to raise awareness about fast fashion

strengths: Insightful discussion of material choice, showing thoughtful research and application of thinking skills
Wellâstructured presentation of ATL skills with clear headings, especially for self-management and thinking skills
Clear explanation of weaving technique implementation, demonstrating practical application of new skills
Creative thinking is evident in how weaving depth evokes sea waves and in connecting technique choices to thematic impact
weaknesses: Source analysis table lacks explicit examples of how research insights informed specific design decisions
Evaluation and analysis tables contain incomplete or inconsistent entries, impeding clarity of how success criteria guided development
Links between ATL thinking skills and concrete design adjustments are not fully drawn
Survey results are presented without acknowledging sample size or demographic limitations, and the bar chart lacks axis titles and a concise caption
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **Applying skills**: The report shows strong awareness of ATL skillsâparticularly in structuring evidence and applying techniques like weavingâwith creative links to the projectâs theme. To deepen this section, the student should complete and refine all tables, explicitly demonstrate how each source influenced design choices, tie every thinking skill back to a criterion, and strengthen data presentation by labeling charts and discussing limitations of survey findings.

strengths: Strong linkage to realâworld contexts through SDG 12 and 13, demonstrating clear relevance and depth of research into sustainable fashion and plastic pollution
Vivid and detailed description of the intended product, outlining both aesthetic and thematic goals for the dress
weaknesses: Learning goal is broad and not narrowly defined or measurable, reducing focus and making success harder to assess
Action plan lacks clear sequencing, deadlines, allocation of responsibilities and required resources, undermining feasibility
Personal interest narrative is incomplete, failing to fully explain how early passion informs the project approach
Success criteria are limited to a single, truncated item; missing measurable benchmarks for aesthetics, materials origin, representation and impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **Planning**: The student effectively grounds the project in meaningful global contexts and paints a compelling picture of the final dress, which highlights strong vision and research. However, without a tightly defined, measurable learning goal or robust success criteria, and with an action plan that omits key logistical details (deadlines, materials, roles) the project risks losing focus and feasibility. Completing the personal interest narrative will also strengthen the connection between motivation and project design.

strengths: Reflective narrative articulates personal growth clearly, linking mastery of new dressmaking techniques to heightened environmental awareness and plans for future practice
weaknesses: Evaluation table assesses only two of the success criteria, leaving the majority unaddressed
Several cells for areas of development are empty, and the final rowâs recommendations are missing
Lacks targeted, specific suggestions for how to improve each success criterion in future iterations
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **Reflecting**: The student presents a thoughtful account of their learning journey and environmental insights, showing genuine self-awareness and growth. However, the reflection would be more robust if every success criterion were systematically evaluated, with each paired development cell filled with concrete improvement strategies, ensuring a balanced and comprehensive self-assessment.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal lists multiple aims but lacks a single, measurable focus. Refine this to one specific outcome (e.g. âconstruct a wearable dress from 100 upcycled plastic pieces by March 1â) to make success criteria clearer.
Strong global context: linking SDG 12 and 13 gives your project clear realâworld relevance and shows depth of research into sustainable fashion and plastic pollution.
Your action plan lists useful tasks but omits clear sequencing and resource allocations. Add specific deadlines, responsible parties and needed materials to improve feasibility.
Your table of source analyses shows selection criteria but lacks evidence of how these informed your process. Add examples of specific insights you applied from each source.
Insightful discussion of material choice and informed decisions demonstrates effective application of research and thinking skills.
Your reflective narrative skillfully articulates personal growth, linking mastery of new techniques to environmental awareness and future practice.
Evaluation table covers only two criteria. For a thorough reflection, complete this table by assessing all success criteria with balanced strengths and areas for growth.
The personal interest section cuts off midâsentence. Complete this narrative to explain how early passion directly shapes your project approach and goals.
Your intended product description is vivid and detailed, clearly outlining aesthetic and thematic goals for the dress as a statement on plastic pollution.
Success criteria table only lists one item. Expand this to include measurable criteria for aesthetics, materials origin, sea representation and impact to fully align with your goals.
The additional success criteria table row is truncated and unclear. Ensure each criterion row is complete, with details and relevance clearly stated.
Clear section heading introducing ATL skills application demonstrates strong structuring of Criterion B evidence.
Your research skills narrative is detailed but would benefit from linking specific sources or findings to concrete design changes in your dress.
Several development cells are empty. Add specific suggestions for improvement against each success criterion to ensure a balanced evaluation.
The evaluation tableâs second row entries are incomplete and inconsistent. Provide full, coherent analysis to show how each criterion guided your design decisions.
Wellâframed section connecting self-management skills directly to product development reflects strong awareness of planning and organization.
Your explanation of weaving technique implementation is clear and shows a good grasp of applying new skills to enhance product quality.
Linking how weaving depth evokes waves effectively demonstrates creative thinking and direct application of ATL thinking skills.
You describe thinking skills use but donât tie specific design adjustments back to success criteria. Explicitly link each decision to one of your success benchmarks.
The final rowâs âareas of developmentâ is missing. Provide targeted recommendations for growth to complete your reflective evaluation.
Survey results are positive, but you should acknowledge sample size and demographic limitations and suggest how this might affect the validity of your conclusions.
The bar chart conveys results but lacks clear axis titles and a concise caption. Label axes explicitly (e.g. âRatingâ) and summarize key findings below the figure.
